@graph.name: Mercedes-Benz C250 Petrol Engine Era Map

mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes-Benz C250 petrol engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Mercedes-Benz C250 petrol engine costs £2,500–£5,000 supply only, varying by generation: M271 £2,500–£4,500, M274 £3,500–£6,000. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,500. A camshaft adjuster repair can cost up to £6,000 at a dealership. Check the Era Map above for your engine code.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes-Benz C250 petrol reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: That depends on which generation. The W204 M271 (2008–2014) is pleasant to drive but has a fragile timing system, with camshaft adjuster and timing chain failures being the defining issues. The W205 M274 (2014–2018) is a significant improvement, offering more power, better torque and superior reliability. Buy the W205 if you can.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes-Benz C250 petrol?

acceptedAnswer.text: W204 M271: camshaft adjuster and timing chain failure (cold-start rattle, £2,000–£6,000), oil consumption (£500–£2,000), ignition coil and injector issues (£200–£600). W205 M274: timing chain stretch (cold-start rattle, £1,500–£3,500).

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes-Benz C250 petrol engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: C-Class values range from £3,000–£20,000 depending on generation, condition and mileage. A £2,500–£5,000 reconditioned engine represents 15–80% of vehicle value — our 70% Mercedes-Benz scrap threshold means careful evaluation is required. For a well-maintained W205, replacement is often justified. For an ageing W204 with other issues, assess scrap value first.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes-Benz C250 petrol engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The C250 petrol has used two engines: M271.860 (W204, 2008–2014, 1.8L turbo, 201 bhp) and M274 DE20 LA (W205, 2014–2018, 2.0L turbo, 211 bhp). Check your VIN or registration year. The code is stamped on the engine block.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the M271 camshaft adjuster issue, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. Camshaft adjuster and timing chain replacement costs £2,000–£6,000 and is significantly cheaper than a full engine replacement (£2,500–£4,500). A worn tensioner can cause the chain to slap, leading to misfires and rough idle. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately — ignored timing issues can lead to catastrophic engine failure.

replacementCosts.h2: Mercedes-Benz C250 Petrol Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect current market ranges across both C250 petrol generations. A camshaft adjuster repair can cost around £6,000 at a dealership, while an engine rebuild typically starts at £2,000–£2,500. See the Era Map above for generation-specific pricing.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a C250 petrol engine typically takes 8–12 hours at £75–£110/hr (independent Mercedes specialist) or £130–£180/hr (London/SE dealer). The M274's direct injection and turbo add complexity.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Mercedes-Benz C250 Petrol — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — if caught before chain snaps

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Mercedes-Benz C250 Petrol? Check These First
buyIf: * W205 (2014–2018) with M274 2.0L — the more reliable engine
* W204 (2010–2014) with documented camshaft adjuster and timing chain history
* Full service history with Mercedes-approved 229.5 spec oil
* Post-2010 W204 — better reliability in terms of timing system
avoidIf: * W204 with any cold-start rattle — timing/camshaft adjuster issue is serious
* W204 with evidence of excessive oil consumption
* Poor service history — both engines are unforgiving of neglect
* W205 with cold-start rattle — timing chain stretch is a risk

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The C250 petrol is a refined and capable executive saloon, but engine choice is critical. The W204 M271 (2008–2014) offers good performance but has well-documented timing chain and camshaft adjuster failures. The W205 M274 (2014–2018) is significantly more reliable with better power delivery and smoothness. Buy the W205 if you can, or a post-2010 W204 with documented timing work. Maintain the oil and timing systems, and the C250 will serve you well.

cta.label: → Compare Mercedes-Benz C250 petrol engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes-Benz C250 Petrol (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for any rattle on startup — M271 camshaft adjuster / M274 timing chain warning
* Service History — Check the year to identify which engine — pre-2014 = M271, 2014+ = M274
* Camshaft Adjuster History — M271: adjusters replaced? Timing chain checked? (critical)
* Oil Consumption — Check oil level; any excessive consumption? (M271 common)
* Ignition System — Any misfires, rough idle, or starting issues? (M271 common)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Cam/crank correlation codes are common
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
